LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ED ANNUNZIATA'S MOTION TO EXTEND DISCOVERY CUTOFF. THE CAUSE IS ARGUED AND SUBMITTED BY BOTH SIDES. THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. THE DISCOVERY CUTOFF WAS SIX WEEKS AGO. DISCOVERY IS NOT THE ONLY MEANS FOR SECURING DOCUMENTS FOR TRIAL; E.G., A NOTICE TO PRODUCE MAY BE SERVED UNDER CCP 1987(C). WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES HUNTER, CSR #6576

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion To Extend Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Aug-08-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Christopher Sargent of Computerlaw Group LLP (650-327-9800) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Joseph S. Belichick of Fenwick & West LLP (650-335-7118) appears as counsel for defendants/opposing parties, Sega of America Inc. and Sega Netowork

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF ED ANNUNZIATA'S Motion To Extend Discovery Cutoff is DENIED. The discovery cutoff was six weeks ago. Discovery is not the only means for securing documents for trial; e.g., a notice to produce may be served under CCP 1987(c). Written order is signed and filed in open court.
